title: Apply for Canadian citizenship: adults and minor children - Canada.ca
content:
## Apply for Canadian citizenship: adults and minor children

- starts when we receive your complete application (no missing information or documents)
- ends when you become a Canadian citizen
- includes the citizenship test and ceremony if applicable
- can be longer if the application is non-routine or complex

Find out how adults (18 and above) and minors (under 18) can become Canadian citizens.
This section replaces the application packages for
- adults (age 18 or older)
- parents or guardians applying for a minor
- minors without a Canadian parent applying alone
Print all pages in this section

## Other citizenship applications
Not sure if this is the right application for you? Find out if you can apply for the following instead:
- Proof of citizenship , which includes passing down citizenship to children born outside Canada replacing lost, destroyed or stolen citizenship certificates updates to your certificate, like name changes
- passing down citizenship to children born outside Canada
- replacing lost, destroyed or stolen citizenship certificates
- updates to your certificate, like name changes
- Canadian Armed Forces members or veterans
- Adopted children born outside Canada
- Stateless people born to a Canadian parent

### Give feedback about this page
